Author: Alexei Panin
Title: Building and capturing value: a CTO’s playbook
Text:
Member-only story
Building and capturing value: a CTO’s playbook
Recently, I had the honor of presenting in front of a group of exceptionally talented students at the Technical University, all of whom are building their own startups. Their sharp questions and fresh perspectives made our conversation so insightful that I felt compelled to register these lessons in this article.
As a startup co-founder and CTO, I’ve come to see technology as an instrument — not the business value at its core. My daily routine revolves around aligning every technical decision with the ultimate goal of not just creating value, but capturing it. In this article, I’ll share why capturing value is as important as creating it, the stories of companies that have mastered the art, and a framework you can use to stress-test your own startup’s capture strategy.
What is value — and who ends up with it?
Value is anything people are willing to trade for — money, time, attention, trust, or data. As founders, our instinct is to solve meaningful problems: build the product, launch the service, deliver something useful. That’s value creation. But creating value doesn’t guarantee you keep it.
Value capture is a separate, critical step: it’s about securing part of what you created — as revenue, influence, customer lock-in, or data ownership. Imagine you build a…
